title: glechoma sibthorpii
intro: Glechoma sibthorpii, also known as Creeping Jenny, is a low-growing, spreading perennial plant in the mint family with small yellow flowers and rounded leaves. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. Its health benefits include reducing anxiety and stress, improving cognitive function, and supporting digestive health, as well as its potential to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. Glechoma sibthorpii is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
